economic_notes: Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc. (100% owned, modified equity method) with total revenue of $2,314,635. Keseechiwan Holdings LP (8.3% interest). Nation has 1905 Surrender Claim and Treaty 4 Agriculture Benefits Claim against Government of Canada. COVID-19 impacted operations; additional government assistance received.
notes: Audit disclaimer of opinion issued — auditor was unable to obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence for a significant number of transactions and for Nakoda Development Corporation. Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 but were signed April 20, 2023 (nearly 2 years late). Four consolidated entities plus one GBE (Nakota Junction Convenience Store Inc.) on modified equity method. Nation has two outstanding land claims (1905 Surrender Claim and Treaty 4 Agriculture Benefits Claim) with related loan financing. Restricted cash of $4,803,698 includes current ($4,061,284) and non-current ($742,414) portions. Funds held in trust of $13,208,027 represent Ottawa Trust Fund monies. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($18,202,618) + FNIHB ($2,270,644) + CMHC ($298,119) = $20,771,381.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
